channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Flexible monolithic 3D complementary circuits based on 2D semiconductor inks
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 22 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-66419-y

2D semiconductors hold promise for the fabrication of high-density flexible integrated circuits, but they often require high-temperature processing or transfer steps. Here, the authors report the low-temperature ( ≤ 150 °C) fabrication of wafer-scale 3Dintegrated flexible complementary circuits based on 2D semiconductor inks.
item.dc:title: Flexible monolithic 3D complementary circuits based on 2D semiconductor inks
item.dc:creator: Taoyu Zou
Seongmin Heo
Youjin Reo
Gi-Seong Ryu
Jaeyun Lee
Mingyu Kim
Soonhyo Kim
Gwon Byeon
Ji-Sang Park
Wentao Xu
Yong-Young Noh

item.title: Wave-mixing cathodoluminescence microscopy of low-frequency excitations
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 22 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67288-1

The authors propose wave-mixing cathodoluminescence, where laser-electron wave mixing in a nonlinear optical cavity upconverts low-frequency molecular resonances into visible photons, enabling nanoscale fingerprinting with visible light sources and detectors.
item.dc:title: Wave-mixing cathodoluminescence microscopy of low-frequency excitations
item.dc:creator: Leila Prelat
Eduardo J. C. Dias
F. Javier García de Abajo
